key_insight: Substituting 50% of Ni with Mn in Cu2NiXY4 kesterite semiconductors preserves the tetragonal structure while modestly narrowing the bandgap across the full X = Sn, Ge, Si and Y = S, Se family.
engineering_relevance: Mn doping offers a tunable route to adjust bandgaps in kesterite chalcogenides, making these materials more adaptable for optoelectronic device design.
method: Performed first-principles DFT calculations on Cu2NiXY4 (X = Sn, Ge, Si; Y = S, Se) compounds.
Used the mBJ+U approach to obtain a more accurate electronic bandgap.
Compared pristine and Mn-substituted systems at the crystallographic 50% substitution limit.
evidence_signal: Across all compounds, the bandgap decreased from 1.028-3.397 eV to 1.007-3.333 eV, with Cu2NiSnS4 dropping from 1.59 eV to 1.49 eV.
watch_item: The study evaluates only a 50% Mn substitution in the kesterite unit cell; lower dopant concentrations require supercell modeling and may change the trends.
paper_title: First-principles Study of Structural and Electronic Properties of Mn-doped Cu2NiXY4 (X=Sn, Ge, Si; Y=S, Se) Chalcogenide Semiconductors
